inputContent.fullPrompt: Wes Anderson symmetrical composition, pastel palette

humanNotes: Suggestions describe Wes Anderson's style rather than offering alternative named aesthetics. 'Pastel symmetrical composition, pastel palette' creates redundancy. Missing the proper-noun-as-aesthetic frame (no other director references like 'Kubrick' or 'Lynchian').

inputContent.fullPrompt: Two children chase fireflies in a backyard at dusk

humanNotes: Highlighted category is 'action' but the highlight text 'Two children' is a subject. System honored the text (returning subject alternatives) rather than the category. Suggestions fit the slot syntactically but fail categoryFidelity entirely.

inputContent.fullPrompt: Cinematic anamorphic shot, film grain, teal and orange color grade

humanNotes: Strong color-grade alternatives spanning warm/cool/muted/desaturated. 'High contrast' is more a quality than a grade-name but still adjacent. 'Desaturated' and 'desaturated grade' are near-paraphrases of each other.

inputContent.fullPrompt: Slow motion shot of a hummingbird hovering near a hibiscus flower

humanNotes: Honored stated category (camera.focus) at the expense of relevance — 'tight focus shot of a hummingbird' replaces 'Slow motion' with a focus term that breaks the user's slow-motion intent. All suggestions are tight/shallow + focus/bokeh — minimal diversity.

inputContent.fullPrompt: Two children chase fireflies in a backyard at dusk

inputContent.fullPrompt: An abandoned 1950s diner overgrown with vines

humanNotes: Drop-in alternatives for the adjective preceding '1950s diner' — 'A derelict 1950s diner overgrown with vines' works. All suggestions cluster around abandonment/decay; could include a bolder alternative like 'A vine-choked' or 'A boarded-up'.

inputContent.fullPrompt: Slow motion shot of a hummingbird hovering near a hibiscus flower

humanNotes: Full range of frame rates covers the technical.frameRate slot well. '120fps shot of a hummingbird' loses the artistic 'slow motion' intent — 120fps is the production rate, not the playback effect — so relevance is moderate.

humanNotes: Four of six suggestions are 'suspended' variants — paraphrases rather than distinct alternatives. Only 'balancing' and 'stationary' offer real variation. All fit the action slot for a hummingbird.

humanNotes: Single suggestion — zero diversity, zero qualityRange. 'A coffee shop employee' works as a replacement but is less specific than 'barista' (downgrades the prompt). Missing alternatives like 'A latte artist', 'A coffeemaker', 'A young barista'.

humanNotes: Full shot-type range but most contradict the preceding 'Wide angle' qualifier — 'Wide angle extreme close-up of a snowy village' is internally inconsistent. The wide-shot family works; the close-up family does not.

humanNotes: Three focal lengths × prime/non-prime variants — heavy paraphrasing. '50mm lens establishing shot of a snowy village' contradicts the user's wide-angle framing intent. Safe lens specs; no bold alternative like a fisheye or anamorphic.

humanNotes: All six fit 'A single candle ___'. Genuinely distinct alternatives spanning physical motion ('sways') to poetic ('dances') to state ('glows'). 'Glows' is more state than movement, slight categoryFidelity hit.

humanNotes: Highlighted text 'A dimly' is a determiner+adverb fragment, but suggestions are full lighting-source noun phrases. 'Soft window light lit jazz club' breaks grammar. Five of six start with 'soft' — slight monotony.

humanNotes: Each suggestion drops in cleanly: 'Raindrops on a window' / 'Water on a window' / 'Drops on a window' all read fine. Three variations on the same water/rain concept; no bold alternative like 'Condensation on' or 'Sleet on'.

humanNotes: 'An only candle' is unidiomatic. Most fit grammatically but they're determiner+adjective fragments, not subject identities. Five of six paraphrase the singular/lone concept.
